NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION, 302 DEFENDANTS' BOARD OF TRUSTEES, SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT, AND CLARA STARR, DIRECTOR OF HUMAN RESOURCES JOINT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND DAMAGES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT BASED ON LACK OF JURISDICTION. FINDINGS MADE IN ARBITRATION OF A GRIEVANCE PURSUANT TO A CBA ARE NOT ENTITLED TO COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL IN THE EMPLOYEE'S LATER LAWSUIT FOR STATUTORY CLAIMS ABSENT (1)A CLEAR AND UNMISTAKABLE WAIVER OF THE EMPLOYEE'S RIGHT TO SUE AND (2)THE ARBITRATION WAS CONDUCTED IN A MANNER THAT ALLOWED FOR A FULL LITIGATION AND FAIR ADJUDICATION OF THE CLAIM. TAYLOR V. LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP. (2003) 113 CAL.APP.4TH 380; CAMARGO V. CALIFORNIA PORTLAND CEMENT CO. (2001) 86 CAL.APP.4TH 995. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (RETALIATION) AND 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (HARASSMENT). TO SUPPORT RETALIATION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT SHOW SHE ENGAGED IN A PROTECTED ACTIVITY, DEFENDANT SUBJECTED HER TO AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION, AND A CAUSAL LINK EXISTS BETWEEN THE PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND DEFENDANT'S ACTION. TO DEMONSTRATE HARASSMENT, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE HARASSMENT WAS SEVERE OR PERVASIVE. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE ACTIONS OF STARR OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF JOB DUTIES WHICH ARE NOT NECESSARY TO PERSONNEL MANAGEMENT. SEE RENO V. BAIRD (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 640. (DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797. (302)

FINDINGS MADE IN ARBITRATION OF A GRIEVANCE PURSUANT TO A CBA ARE NOT ENTITLED TO COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL IN THE EMPLOYEE'S LATER LAWSUIT FOR STATUTORY CLAIMS ABSENT (1)A CLEAR AND UNMISTAKABLE WAIVER OF THE EMPLOYEE'S RIGHT TO SUE AND (2)THE ARBITRATION WAS CONDUCTED IN A MANNER THAT ALLOWED FOR A FULL LITIGATION AND FAIR ADJUDICATION OF THE CLAIM. TAYLOR V. LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP. (2003) 113 CAL.APP.4TH 380; CAMARGO V. CALIFORNIA PORTLAND CEMENT CO. (2001) 86 CAL.APP.4TH 995. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (RETALIATION) AND 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (HARASSMENT). TO SUPPORT RETALIATION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT SHOW SHE ENGAGED IN A PROTECTED ACTIVITY, DEFENDANT SUBJECTED HER TO AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION, AND A CAUSAL LINK EXISTS BETWEEN THE PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND DEFENDANT'S ACTION. TO DEMONSTRATE HARASSMENT, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE HARASSMENT WAS SEVERE OR PERVASIVE. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE ACTIONS OF STARR OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF JOB DUTIES WHICH ARE NOT NECESSARY TO PERSONNEL MANAGEMENT. SEE RENO V. BAIRD (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 640. (Defendant/moving party to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
